extraction.one_line_summary: POGACHAR Tadej won from a five-rider sprint after surviving a mid-race mechanical at 65km to go, timing his sprint from last wheel to edge ALAPHILIPPE Julian by three-quarters of a length.
tactical_patterns: VAN AVERMAET Greg attacked from distance with VANDERSAN Tosh to force favorites into chasing, exploiting Lotto's breakaway rider.
POGACHAR mechanical at 65km forced desperate chase entering hardest section, yet UAE controlled finale group completely despite early chaos.
Five-rider sprint group stayed tactically bunched through final kilometer, ALAPHILIPPE forced to front too early around 300m enabling last-wheel advantage.
WOODS Michael and GAUDU David outsprinted VALVERDE Alejandro despite Valverde leading final corners, revealing sprint hierarchy among climber types.

rider_signals.POGACHAR Tadej: positive: survived mechanical at 65km into hardest climbs, then won sprint from last wheel position showing exceptional freshness from skipping midweek race
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: fastest in chase group but tactically forced to front 300m out, losing positional advantage despite relegation lesson from previous year
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: positive: led out final kilometer work yet still had legs for fourth, strongest Canadian ride showing climber-sprinter versatility
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: neutral: attacked from distance early to force tempo but ineffective spring showing AG2R investment yet to pay dividends
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: led final corners but faded to fifth when sprint opened, tactical positioning wasted without finishing speed
